name: Black Bean Sandwich Torte
recipeIngredient: 15 ounces canned black beans, drained and rinsed
2 large fresh lavash, 10-12 inch pieces (or pita)
3 medium ripe tomatoes, about 12 ounces, sliced into 1/2-inch thick pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the Black Bean Spread, place the black beans in the bowl of a food processor fitted with a chopping blade. In a small skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat; add the garlic, oregano, and red pepper. Cook for 2 minutes.
2. Add the garlic mixture (including all the oil) to the beans. Add the vinegar and salt; process until the mixture is almost a smooth puree. Set aside.
3. To assemble the torte, fit a 22-inch piece of aluminum foil into a 9-inch square baking pan so that it covers the bottom and overhangs opposite sides. Cut out a 9-inch square from one lavash piece, reserving trimmings. Place the lavash in the foil-lined pan and top with the trimmings as evenly as possible.
4. Preheat oven to 250°F. Spoon Black Bean Spread evenly over the lavash. Top with layers of arugula, feta, red onion, and tomato slices.
5. Cut out another 9-inch square from remaining lavash and place over the tomato layer. Fold in the overhanging foil sides to cover the torte. Bake for 15 minutes to warm.
6. Remove the torte from the pan by lifting the foil ends. Slide it off the foil onto a cutting surface with a spatula. Cut into quarters, then cut each quarter diagonally in half and serve.
(Note: Fresh lavash is also known as "shepherd's bread," similar to pita but thinner and larger. If unavailable, substitute Afghan bread, large flour tortillas, or packaged thin-crust pizza dough like Boboli.)
